title: Tropical Algerian Cacao Tagine
description: A creamy and kid-friendly Algerian tagine that blends coconut milk, cacao, and apricots into a sweet spicy dish, finished with a safe flambé flair.

ingredients.name: coconut milk
ingredients.notes: creamy texture, season with 1/2 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper, and 1 tbsp lemon juice

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a tagine or deep saucepan over medium heat.
Add diced onion and minced garlic, sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in sliced zucchini and bell pepper, cook 4 minutes until slightly tender.
Add cooked chickpeas and pour in coconut milk, stir to combine.
Whisk in cacao powder and honey, then fold in sliced apricots.
Sprinkle ground cinnamon and cumin, stir well.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the lid, swirl the mixture, then lightly pour a splash of rum and ignite carefully to flambe.
Let the flames subside, then simmer uncovered for an additional 5 minutes to deepen flavors.
Stir in a splash of lemon juice just before serving for brightness.
Transfer to an agave leaf vessel to serve, garnish with fresh mint if desired.
Allow to sit for 2 minutes before scooping into bowls.
